name: Pickled Jalapenos-ch
recipeIngredient: 1 Whole bay leaf, up to 2 leaves
2 Whole peppercorns, up to 1 small piece cinnamon
recipeInstructions: To preserve jalapenos or any other chiles, use this recipe adapted from a high school cooking class in Mexico. Slice carrots into rounds, break cauliflower into small pieces, and cut onions into chunks. Halve garlic cloves lengthwise. Use whole chiles but puncture them with a toothpick to release flavor. Heat oil (3-4 tablespoons) and combine vegetables. Fill jars with vegetables, adding spices and salt. Heat vinegar until boiling, then pour over vegetables. Seal jars tightly and boil for 15 minutes. Allow pickles to stand at least one week before consuming. Pickled jalapenos preserved in this way can be used long-term by adding fresh chiles as needed.
